props.message:
The Ledger.

The running record of the realm. Speak softly — the scribes are listening for inscriptions.

A pinned snippet, recently inked:

"...the seventh wardstone in Sigilkeep dimmed at the third hour of the morning, and brightened at the fourth, with no walker seen near it."

A folded notice, weighted with a stone:

"NOTICE. An unusually large number of un-quieted appeared in the Murk last cycle. The Hidden Choir is consulted. Walkers are advised to keep to lit paths."

A wood plaque polished by hands:

"In memory of the Senior Scribes — Vael the elder, Anth the long-handed, Tarn who kept the longest scroll, and Pell the first."

A small inscription card:

"...and on the morning of the fourth thinning, the Source was held for the length of one breath, and three Readers fainted, and the realm continued."

A loose page held under a stone weight:

"...a child in Hearthold asked where her grandfather was buried. The Senior Scribe noted the question. The answer was not given."

A small marker pinned to a beam:

"Inscriptions from the Burnt Hollow have been arriving in a hand none of us writes. We copy them faithfully. We do not ask."

A weather-stained note:

"...the rooks of Hearthold sang at first light, and the Source was full in the air, and a small walker passed under the gate. We marked the line. Walk well."

An old proclamation, framed:

"By the keepers of the Ledger: the running scroll shall not be cut. What is begun is recorded entire. Brevity is for kinder rooms."

props.open_message: A handful of small pebbles, set aside for some scribe's tally.
A pressed heart-flower, the petals still soft. Whoever set this here did not write a note.
A small dark key. The Ledger keeps these for its inner cabinets.

root.speaker: Senior Scribe Vonn
root.text: (He looks up from a scroll that runs off the desk and onto the floor.) Mm. A walker. What can the Ledger do for you?

ledger.speaker: Senior Scribe Vonn
ledger.text: The running record of the realm. Every birth, every door opened, every shrine renewed, every un-quieted in the Murk. We copy what arrives. We do not write what is not given to us.

volume.speaker: Senior Scribe Vonn
volume.text: Lirien's breath, walker — they never stop arriving. Every breath of every Wyrdkin, every footfall, every cooking pot. We keep only the ones the Hidden Choir marks. Even that is too many.

who.speaker: Senior Scribe Vonn
who.text: We scribes copy. The marking, the choosing of what to copy — that is older work, done by older voices. We do not see them. They leave the inscriptions on the desk overnight, in a hand none of us writes.

bye.speaker: Senior Scribe Vonn
bye.text: Walk well. If you do anything noteworthy, we will hear of it. Try not to make our scrolls any longer.

root.speaker: A grey Reader
root.text: Walker. You are scheduled. I have logged you. Pleased to meet you.

scheduled.speaker: A grey Reader
scheduled.text: (She looks at you with a precise small smile. She does not answer the question. She tilts her head a little, as though listening to something.) The Wyrdmark walks. We attend.

logged.speaker: A grey Reader
logged.text: An old habit of speech. Forgive it. Some of the older Readers had the habit too. They worked here a long time.

bye.speaker: A grey Reader
bye.text: Walk well, walker. We have your line.

root.speaker: Scribe Penn
root.text: Hey — sorry, you startled me. Have you been in the realm lately? Have you noticed anything... I don't know. Strange?

mean.speaker: Scribe Penn
mean.text: The inscriptions arriving lately have been... uneven. Long days of nothing. Then a flood at once. The Senior Scribe says it's normal. I don't think it is.

breath.speaker: Scribe Penn
breath.text: The breath has been odd. Walking out at dawn, sometimes the Source feels — held. As if Lirien were paying close attention to something else and not quite to us. Khorgaul's grip, maybe I'm imagining it.

bye.speaker: Scribe Penn
bye.text: Walk well. If you do see anything — come back and tell us. We'll write it down.
